sourceOfTruth: Portfolio Item source data for the Learning Portfolio Site.

caseStudies.title: Employee Assessment Bootcamp: Observation to Individual Reporting
caseStudies.description: Connects a multi-day behavioral observation workbook with a redacted individual report to show the assessment flow from evidence capture and scoring to participant-facing synthesis.
caseStudies.summary: This case study shows an end-to-end employee assessment workflow: competency mapping, activity-specific observation, anchored scoring, evidence calibration, consolidated results, and an anonymized individual report.

image.alt: Anonymized employee assessment bootcamp team photo

reviewerContext.label: Use case
reviewerContext.value: Review behavioral assessment design, observation structure, scoring logic, and participant-facing reporting.

reviewerContext.label: Scope
reviewerContext.value: Competency mapping, multi-day observation, assignments, peer input, calibration, score consolidation, and individual reporting.

reviewerContext.label: Evidence limit
reviewerContext.value: The artifacts demonstrate assessment structure and reporting quality. They do not independently validate the participant scores or program impact.

caseFlow.label: Map assessment evidence
caseFlow.value: Connect behavior dimensions to activities, methods, indicators, and anchored rubrics.

caseFlow.label: Capture observable behavior
caseFlow.value: Record concrete actions and statements across discussions, assignments, coaching, simulations, and peer review.

caseFlow.label: Calibrate and consolidate
caseFlow.value: Review evidence quality, align scoring, and combine activity results into dimension-level findings.

caseFlow.label: Translate results
caseFlow.value: Convert the consolidated evidence into a concise individual report with scores, strengths, and development priorities.

artifacts.title: Employee Assessment Observation Workbook
artifacts.description: A redacted multi-sheet workbook for competency mapping, activity observation, scoring rubrics, daily recaps, and consolidated results.

artifacts.title: Employee Assessment Individual Report
artifacts.description: A redacted participant-facing report that combines dimension scores, executive summaries, strengths, and development priorities.

caseStudies.title: Competency-Based Administrative Communication Learning Program
caseStudies.portfolioItemTitle: Administrative Communication Learning Program Case Study
caseStudies.description: Combines needs analysis, proposal, lesson plan, facilitator material, and evaluation tools into one competency-based workplace communication learning case.
caseStudies.summary: This case study consolidates the administrative communication portfolio items into one end-to-end learning design case: diagnosis, proposal, lesson plan, facilitator material, and evaluation tools.

caseStudies.discussUrl: contact.html?portfolioItem=Administrative Communication Learning Program Case Study

reviewerContext.label: Use case
reviewerContext.value: Review competency-based learning design capability for workplace communication.

reviewerContext.label: Scope
reviewerContext.value: Training needs analysis, program proposal, session design, learning material, post-test, reaction form, observation form, and performance rubric.

reviewerContext.label: Evidence limit
reviewerContext.value: The artifacts show design quality and measurement structure. They do not independently prove learner outcome impact.

caseFlow.label: Diagnose the gap
caseFlow.value: Identify administrative communication needs and translate them into practical training priorities.

caseFlow.label: Design the program
caseFlow.value: Convert the need into a competency-based proposal and 36-hour lesson plan with sessions, activities, OJT practice, and evidence requirements.

caseFlow.label: Build learning support
caseFlow.value: Prepare facilitator-ready material for confident, professional, and structured workplace communication.

caseFlow.label: Measure quality
caseFlow.value: Use post-test, reaction feedback, observation, and rubric tools to evaluate understanding, experience, and behavior transfer.

reviewerContext.label: Use case
reviewerContext.value: Review learning strategy, curriculum architecture, analytics, and decision-support capability.

reviewerContext.label: Scope
reviewerContext.value: Macro needs analysis, competency matrix, competency-based syllabus, learning business plan, marketing plan, budget and ROTI model, and Kirkpatrick Level 1 and Level 2 dashboards.

reviewerContext.label: Evidence limit
reviewerContext.value: The artifacts show planning logic, measurement design, and analysis structure. They do not independently prove commercial or learner outcome impact.

caseFlow.label: Read demand signals
caseFlow.value: Synthesize labor-market and workplace signals into future-ready training priorities.

caseFlow.label: Map capability
caseFlow.value: Convert priorities into a competency matrix and syllabus that make program readiness visible.

caseFlow.label: Frame the business
caseFlow.value: Connect capability priorities to learning services, positioning, demand generation, and client retention logic.

caseFlow.label: Model investment
caseFlow.value: Use budget and ROTI logic to make training investment decisions easier to compare.

caseFlow.label: Evaluate learning
caseFlow.value: Convert reaction and learning data into dashboards for program improvement decisions.

caseStudies.title: Youth Mentoring Workbook and Final Pitch Readiness System
caseStudies.portfolioItemTitle: Youth Mentoring Workbook and Pitch Readiness Case Study
caseStudies.description: Combines three mentoring workbooks and a final pitch deck template into one staged youth project mentoring case.
caseStudies.summary: This case study consolidates a four-week mentoring sequence: idea exploration, concept development, presentation readiness, and final pitch deck structure.

image.alt: Youth mentoring workbook and pitch readiness case study thumbnail

caseStudies.discussUrl: contact.html?portfolioItem=Youth Mentoring Workbook and Pitch Readiness Case Study

reviewerContext.label: Use case
reviewerContext.value: Review mentoring program design for a student or youth project team moving from idea selection to final pitch readiness.

reviewerContext.label: Scope
reviewerContext.value: Week 1 idea exploration workbook, Week 2 concept development workbook, Week 3 presentation readiness workbook, and Week 4 pitch deck template.

reviewerContext.label: Evidence limit
reviewerContext.value: The artifacts show mentoring structure, reflection prompts, project planning logic, and pitch-readiness support. They do not independently prove final project outcomes.

caseFlow.label: Explore ideas
caseFlow.value: Guide mentees from individual SDG interests into shared problem statements and early project directions.

caseFlow.label: Develop the concept
caseFlow.value: Use voting, feasibility checks, and project canvas prompts to converge on one workable concept.

caseFlow.label: Prepare the presentation
caseFlow.value: Track evidence, ownership, storytelling, and slide readiness before the final pitch.

caseFlow.label: Package the pitch
caseFlow.value: Use a structured deck template to present problem, evidence, solution, impact, implementation, and sustainability.
